extraction.one_line_summary: WOUT VAN AERT won the 40km time trial with a hat-trick, while VINGEGAARD cruised to secure yellow, even slowing in final meters.
tactical_patterns: Van Aert went sub-48 minutes at 51kph average, the only rider faster than 48 minutes showing sprinter-palmares riders can peak TT.
Vingegaard deliberately slowed final 200m after wobble on descent, prioritizing GC safety over stage hunting with huge margin.
Vlasov biggest GC top-10 mover via TT, exploiting 40km+ distance unsuited to pure climbers in mountainous Tours.
Bissegger's gearing problems requiring bike change shows equipment choice gambles backfire on varied terrain long TTs.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Third stage win of Tour, sub-48 at 51kph proves he can dominate long TTs not just prologues.
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: Strong ride but 40 seconds down to Van Aert suggests Grand Tour fatigue limits his usual TT dominance.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Faster than Pogacar at early time checks despite coasting to finish, showing comfort managing big GC leads.
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: Lost time to Vingegaard even in a TT where he was pre-stage favorite alongside Ganna, confirming GC defeat.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Challenged for stage win until final 8km climb, revealing climbing weakness in long varied-terrain TTs.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Moved into top-5 GC via TT, biggest top-10 mover showing Russian school TT strength underestimated.
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: negative: Gearing miscalculation forced bike change destroying podium chances, tactical preparation error costly.
